steps.title: Check the impossible region
steps.content: If $x<0$, then $\frac{1}{3}x-2$ is less than $-2$. The inequality requires $y$ to be even smaller than that, so no point with $y>0$ can satisfy it. This rules out the region with negative $x$-values and positive $y$-values.

steps.title: Match the region
steps.content: The other listed regions can contain points below the boundary line, but this one cannot. The region with $x<0$ and $y>0$ contains no solution points. The answer is A.
